title: Long-running transaction delayed migration cutover
failure_mechanism: A long-running transaction held a conflicting lock while a migration attempted cutover, causing dependent authentication writes and audit-event processing to wait rather than fail as a consumer-capacity problem.
mitigation_summary: Service behavior normalized after the lock conflict was no longer present and database write latency returned to its ordinary range; no action is implied for a new incident.
timeline_summary: The migration began before authentication write latency rose. Database wait evidence appeared while worker health remained stable and audit-event delay followed the write-path slowdown.
narrative_safe: RelayOps authentication writes slowed after a migration entered cutover while a long-running transaction remained active. The resulting audit-event delay may resemble a queue-consumer issue, but no consumer error pattern was observed.
